clusters.root_cause_summary: The stair centroid pixel lies in a navmesh-disconnected component, causing Reach_stair_centroid to remain False on every gcts step and the stair to never be traversed. No waypoint-adjustment mechanism applied after Phase 1 entry can resolve a geometric disconnection; the fix must prevent engagement with the unreachable centroid before Phase 1 commits.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Identical across both scenes: stair centroid coordinates fall outside the connected navmesh component reachable from the robot; Reach_stair_centroid: False fires on every gcts step; no floor switch occurs; DTG never drops below ~1.0m; the frontier associated with the stair is disabled without traversal. Differences: q3zU7Yy5E5s targets an UPSTAIR centroid (Phase 1 entry via look_for_downstair at steps 70–81), and all 6 T6 interception attempts produced byte-identical behavioral fingerprints — the centroid disconnection is far from any navigable boundary. qyAac8rV8Zk targets a DOWNSTAIR centroid (Phase 2), and the T6 baseline's 0.4m pullback moved the Phase 2 waypoint to a robot-adjacent navigable pixel, achieving SUCCESS (DTG=0.094m, step 415) — the centroid is only marginally disconnected.
clusters.transfer_notes: The T6 baseline solving qyAac8rV8Zk reveals that a 0.4m pullback toward the robot is sufficient when the centroid is marginally disconnected (downstair geometry). This does NOT transfer to q3zU7Yy5E5s: the same 0.4m/0.5m pullback, BFS snaps, ring-sampling, CC-validated carrot, and ±50px navmesh box checks all return byte-identical behavioral fingerprints — the upstair centroid is sufficiently far from any navigable pixel that no waypoint perturbation within stair bounding-box range lands on the connected component. The transfer insight is directional: fix qyAac8rV8Zk by restoring the 0.4m pullback (already proven); fix q3zU7Yy5E5s by blocking centroid registration upstream, before Phase 1 is ever entered.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: Compound fix: (1) For q3zU7Yy5E5s — inject a strict single-pixel navigability check at the exact centroid coordinates at stair registration time (on_episode_start or post_episode_init), disabling the upstair centroid entry if navigable_map[centroid_row, centroid_col] == 0, preventing Phase 1 from ever being entered for a geometrically unreachable upstair; fall through to frontier-only exploration. (2) For qyAac8rV8Zk — restore DP9 to 0.4m pullback as sole carrot mechanism (revert candidate_1's 0.6m forward projection), which the T6 baseline proved sufficient. Both can be delivered in a single candidate: centroid_navigability_gate_at_registration + DP9_0.4m_pullback.

clusters.root_cause_summary: XB4GS9ShBRE has two layered failure modes: (1) the 0.6m forward-projection carrot shortens Phase 2 traversal depth, causing stair exit at a frontier-poor second-floor boundary position and immediate 'no unexplored frontiers' at floor_step=1; (2) when traversal is deep enough (candidate_0, three paused_step resets), a false-positive STOP fires at step 499 during initialize mode with floor_step=1 and DTG=0.131m, preventing final goal approach.
clusters.cross_scene_pattern: Single scene; two sub-mechanisms interact sequentially. Sub-mechanism 1 (traversal depth) is upstream: carrot distance determines how far into the stair the robot penetrates during Phase 2, which determines the richness of the frontier supply on the second floor post-traversal. Sub-mechanism 2 (false-positive STOP) is downstream: it only becomes the binding failure when sub-mechanism 1 is mitigated (candidate_0 exposed it; candidate_1 masked it by reinstating sub-mechanism 1). The STOP fires in initialize mode at floor_step=1 — a reinit step — not during normal exploration, indicating the should_stop gate does not distinguish between a settled exploration step and a floor reinit step.
clusters.transfer_notes: Candidate_0's three paused_step resets (11→19→0→1→0→SUCCESS@1) confirm that the Phase 2 traversal under the 0.4m pullback exits at a frontier-rich second-floor position, enabling 280+ explore steps. This establishes that restoring 0.4m pullback (no forward projection) is a prerequisite for exposing sub-mechanism 2. The false-positive STOP at step 499 (initialize mode, floor_step=1, DTG=0.131m) is structurally distinct from the navmesh-disconnection failures in q3zU7Yy5E5s/qyAac8rV8Zk — it is a stop-gate miscalibration during floor reinit, not a stair reachability problem. No cross-scene transfer applies.
clusters.highest_leverage_untested: suppress_STOP_when_mode_is_initialize_and_floor_step_lt_20: gate the should_stop / STOP action on floor_step >= 20, preventing false-positive SUCCESS during the initialize reinit window (candidate_0 step 499, floor_step=1). Combine with restoring DP9 to 0.4m pullback (no forward projection) to ensure Phase 2 exits at a frontier-rich second-floor position, re-enabling the 280+ explore steps that candidate_0 achieved before hitting the false-positive. Both changes are required jointly: the stop guard is irrelevant unless deep traversal is first restored.

forbidden_moves.reason: Moves the Phase 2 waypoint back into the disconnected navmesh region for qyAac8rV8Zk — candidate_1 confirmed: Reach_stair_centroid False throughout, min_dis_to_downstair oscillating 160–166, identical to all pre-T6 failures. Has no beneficial effect on q3zU7Yy5E5s whose centroid is disconnected regardless of projection direction.

Shortens Phase 2 traversal depth in XB4GS9ShBRE — candidate_1 confirmed regression from DTG=0.131m (candidate_0, deep traversal) to DTG=0.683m (candidate_1, stair exit at frontier-poor boundary position, no-frontier fires at floor_step=1).

The ±50px box around the q3zU7Yy5E5s upstair centroid expands into the connected navmesh boundary, returning centroid_navigable=True and leaving Phase 1 entirely unchanged. Confirmed: [T6_FLOOR_NAVCHECK] tag never appeared in logs; behavioral fingerprint byte-identical to candidate_0 across all 5 prior candidates tested with box-based approaches.

All gcts_streak thresholds tested (N=8, 10, 30) for q3zU7Yy5E5s produce byte-identical behavioral fingerprints. The interception fires after Phase 1 is already committed to an unreachable centroid, and the redirect target is also outside the connected component; the fix must occur before Phase 1 entry, not during gcts stall.

Global forbidden move. The failure mode for both scenes is not a premature SUCCESS — it is complete failure to traverse the stair (Reach_stair_centroid always False, no floor switch). The guard addresses a distinct failure class (early-exit SUCCESS during stair traversal) that is not triggered here.

Global forbidden move. XB4GS9ShBRE's false-positive is a STOP action during initialize mode (step 499, floor_step=1) triggered via the should_stop path, not a premature stair_climb SUCCESS in the Phase 2 paused_step gate. These are different code paths.

Frontier exhaustion in XB4GS9ShBRE candidate_1 fires at floor_step=1 immediately after Phase 2 exit because the traversal is too shallow — the robot exits at a second-floor boundary with no local frontiers. Increasing seeding radius cannot compensate for placement at a geometrically frontier-poor exit point; the fix is deeper traversal (restore 0.4m carrot), not wider seeding.

Global forbidden move. DP12 gates one floor-switch reinit path; the binding failure is navmesh disconnection preventing stair traversal entirely — no DP12 value affects whether Reach_stair_centroid fires True or False.
Global forbidden move. Phase 2 traversal depth and the false-positive STOP during initialize mode are not gated by DP12's floor-switch interval.
